nodes.en: Architecture designs and realizes buildings, spaces, and urban systems that respond to human needs, environmental conditions, and aesthetic principles.
nodes.cs: Architektura navrhuje a realizuje budovy, prostory a městské systémy reagující na lidské potřeby, environmentální podmínky a estetické principy.
nodes.brief:
Architecture designs and realizes buildings, spaces, and urban systems that respond to human needs, environmental conditions, and aesthetic principles. It works at scales from a single dwelling to whole districts, balancing structural feasibility, climate and site response, regulatory codes, and the cultural and symbolic meaning a building carries for the people who use it — the concern that distinguishes it from disciplines that solve the same physical problems purely on engineering terms.

Architecture sits inside [[03]] Applied Sciences & Technology beside [[03.01]] Engineering, on whose structural, mechanical, and electrical disciplines it depends to make a design physically buildable, and [[03.05]] Manufacturing, which supplies the prefabricated components and materials increasingly used in modern construction. Unlike most of its siblings, architecture has no dedicated subcategory tree in this taxonomy yet — its output is judged as a single integrated discipline rather than divided into structural, urban, and interior specializations.

Architectural practice is governed by building codes, licensure, and — for larger projects — urban planning regulation. For professional standards and practice guidance, see the [American Institute of Architects](https://www.aia.org/), and for a global reference on architectural history and theory, the [Encyclopaedia Britannica](https://www.britannica.com/topic/architecture).
